title: The Dried Mercy Bend

features.name: mercy shed
features.desc-on-touch: A corrugated iron shed where the river-keeper stored measurement tools. The corruption has filed the tools as local history; now they measure time instead of depth.

features.name: dry pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where the river pooled before the drought. The silt holds the shape of water; you can see where the current spiralled. The archive is learning to store memory in these curves.

features.name: reading the bend
features.desc-on-touch: A standing stone carved by no human hand—by the river's own erosion. It reads like a sentence unlearning how to end.

features.name: riverbed scar
features.desc-on-touch: The dried bed itself—a long linear wound through the paddock. You touch it and feel what it holds: water was here, flow was here, and both are still remembered.

features.name: ghost current
features.desc-on-touch: A shimmer in the air where water once moved. The archive's sensors detect flow still, though nothing flows. The path continues even in absence.

inscription: the river learns that running was only the beginning; staying is the slower comparison

file.name: Mercy_Bend_Hydrology_Thesis_Incomplete.txt
file.note: A study of how the riverbed learns to hold memory in dried curves. Footnotes have filed themselves as separate entities, each remembering what the main text forgot.

exits.label: downriver, where the bed becomes words
